message:
perf(output/toon): short-circuit toon string sanitization on ASCII bytes

The old sanitize_toon_string clean-string fast path called .chars() and
.is_control() over every input — fine for short titles, but the toon
output emits issue.message and issue.subject for every row, so the
allocation-free path was paying for full UTF-8 decode + Unicode
property lookup on what is overwhelmingly ASCII.

ascii_toon_string_is_clean() walks bytes instead of chars:

- Returns Some(false) immediately on a control byte (0x00..=0x1f
  except \n/\t, plus 0x7f) — the slow escape path picks up.
- Returns Some(true) for pure-ASCII strings with no control bytes —
  caller borrows the input directly.
- Returns None if any non-ASCII byte (0x80..=0xff) was seen — caller
  falls back to the original char-aware check, since determining
  whether a multi-byte sequence is a control is_control() char
  requires actually decoding it.

The None fallback path preserves the exact previous behaviour, so
this is purely a speed-up on the common ASCII case (most issue
metadata) without changing semantics on pages of non-ASCII text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
